THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. DUE TO THE IMPENDING TRIAL DATE, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JANUARY 7, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ITS MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 21, 2012 AT 4 PM. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARKMONITOR, INC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATIVELY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS ENTITLED TO COMMISSION. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IS NOT SUPERFLUOUS, AND PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED BAD FAITH CONDUCT OF DEFENDANT. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF WAS AN EXEMPT EMPLOYEE BECAUSE IT HAD REASONABLE AND REALISTIC EXPECTATIONS THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD SPEND MORE TIME OUTSIDE OF THE OFFICE. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF AS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THOSE EXPECTATIONS WERE COMMUNICATED TO HIM. AS TO THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTIONS REGARDING MEAL BREAKS AND REST BREAKS, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRAIBLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER HIS RIGHT TO TAKE MEAL AND REST BREAKS WERE COMMUNICATED TO HIM. DEFENDANT CONCEDES THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION OF VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE SECTIONS 201 AND 203 IS DERIVATIVE OF THE PREVIOUS LABOR CODE CLAIMS, AND ARE DENIED FOR THE SAME REASONS. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT FAILED TO PAY PLAINTIFF ALL WAGES THAT WERE DUE UPON TERMINATION. DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS PREDICATED ON NAHLINDER'S LABOR CODE CLAIMS DISCUSSED ABOVE AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATIONS IS DENIED FOR THOSE REASONS. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED AND SIGNED BY THE COURT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARKMONITOR, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF MATS NAHLINDER; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. SUBSEQUENT DISCOVERY HAS REVEALED THAT THE DOCUMENT PRODUCTION MADE AT THE TIME OF PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION DID NOT INCLUDE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS AND INFORMATION THAT WERE REASONABLY AVAILABLE OR KNOWN AT THE TIME OF THE ORIGINAL DEPOSITION. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION WILL BE REOPENED FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF QUESTIONING PLAINTIFF ON EMAILS IN HIS SUPPLEMENTAL PRODUCTION, HIS FAILURE TO PRODUCE THE EMAILS IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S OCTOBER 2011 DISCOVERY REQUESTS, ANY OTHER MARKMONITOR DOCUMENTS IN HIS POSSESSION AND ANY ISSUES ARISING FROM THESE TOPICS. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Defendant Markmonitor, Incorporated'S Motion To Re-Open The Deposition Of Pltf Mats Nahlinder; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARVIN DUNSON III, ESQ., 650.353.6446, FOR PLAINTIFF MATS NAHLINDER.
KAI-CHING CHA, ESQ., AND AMANDA N. FU, ESQ., 415.399.8419 AND 415.439.6252, FOR DEFENDANT MARKMONITOR, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant MarkMonitor, Incorporated's Motion To Re-Open The Deposition Of Plaintiff Mats Nahlinder; Request For Sanctions is granted for good cause shown. Subsequent discovery has revealed that the document production made at the time of Plaintiff's deposition did not include all responsive documents and information that were reasonably available or known at the time of the original deposition. Plaintiff's deposition will be reopened for the limited purpose of questioning Plaintiff on emails in his supplemental production, his failure to produce the emails in response to Defendant's October 2011 discovery requests, any other MarkMonitor documents in his possession and any issues arising from these topics. Monetary sanctions are denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARRIN DUNSON, TEL. NO. (650) 353-6446 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN C. KLOOSTERMAN OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 677-3128 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.
RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT MARKMONITOR, INC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATIVELY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS ENTITLED TO COMMISSION. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IS NOT SUPERFLUOUS, AND PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED BAD FAITH CONDUCT OF DEFENDANT. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF WAS AN EXEMPT EMPLOYEE BECAUSE IT HAD REASONABLE AND REALISTIC EXPECTATIONS THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD SPEND MORE TIME OUTSIDE OF THE OFFICE. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF AS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THOSE EXPECTATIONS WERE COMMUNICATED TO HIM. AS TO THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTIONS REGARDING MEAL BREAKS AND REST BREAKS, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRAIBLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER HIS RIGHT TO TAKE MEAL AND REST BREAKS WERE COMMUNICATED TO HIM. DEFENDANT CONCEDES THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION OF VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE SECTIONS 201 AND 203 IS DERIVATIVE OF THE PREVIOUS LABOR CODE CLAIMS, AND ARE DENIED FOR THE SAME REASONS. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT FAILED TO PAY PLAINTIFF ALL WAGES THAT WERE DUE UPON TERMINATION. DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS PREDICATED ON NAHLINDER'S LABOR CODE CLAIMS DISCUSSED ABOVE AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATIONS IS DENIED FOR THOSE REASONS. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED AND SIGNED BY THE COURT. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/MJM)

AMANDA FU, ESQ., 415.439.1940, FOR DEFENDANT MARKMONITOR, INC.

Parties have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED/DISCUSSED. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT/DISCUSSION, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Markmonitor, Inc.'S Motion For Protective Order Limiting Scope Of Plaintiff'S Noticed Depositions IS GRANTED IN PART. DEPOSITION TOPICS AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS NARROWED. DEPOSITIONS OF PRECIPIENT WITNESSES TO OCCUR BY JANUARY 16, 2013.
